locationConfirm: The location found for coordinates `{latitude:string},{longitude:string}` is **{location:string}**.
**Note:** This location may have been generalized to a known location if it was located outside of the regions covered by the [National Weather Service](https://weather.gov).
locationConfirmAdjusted: The coordinates `{requestedLatitude:string},{requestedLongitude:string}` are not directly supported by the [National Weather Service](https://weather.gov).
Coordinates have been resolved to `{latitude:string},{longitude:string}` for **{location:string}**.
Would you like to use this location?
locationConfirmAdjustedWithImage: The coordinates `{requestedLatitude:string},{requestedLongitude:string}` are not directly supported by the [National Weather Service](https://weather.gov).
Coordinates have been resolved to `{latitude:string},{longitude:string}` for **{location:string}** with the radar image below.
Would you like to use this location?

notices.promptTimedOut: You did not respond to the prompt in time.

helpText: - Use the {alertsAdd:string} command to add a weather alert reporting destination
- Use the {forecasts:string} command to designate a channel for reporting hourly forecasts to
- Use the {autoRadar:string} command to designate a channel to keep an auto-updating doppler radar animation in
- Use the {radar:string} command to get the current doppler radar animation for a location
Admins in this server can use the {announcementSubscribe:string} command to set a channel to receive announcements related to my operation. Announcements are opt-in as to reduce noise.

errors.invalidCoordinates: The provided latitude or longitude is not valid.
errors.noDestinationsForType: There are no {type:string} destinations in this server.
errors.tooManyDestinationsForType: You have exceeded the max limit of {type:string} destinations (`{max:number}`).
locationNotFound: The location you specified could not be found by the National Weather Service.
__Make sure the coordinates you provide are those of locations in the United States of America,__ for example latitude `21.3062945` and longitude `-157.8555066` for a location in Hawaii.
locationLookupHttpError: There was a problem retrieving the location you provided.```{errorCode:number}: {errorStatus:string}```
Only locations within the United States of America are valid.

introMessage: ## Thank you for adding me to your server! Here's a quick rundown to get started:
Lastly, if you encounter any bugs, oddities, or have any suggestions then use the {feedbackSubmit:string} command to submit feedback!
-# I apologize if this is not an appropriate channel for this introduction.

create.commandFailed: I've encountered a problem executing that command. Please try again later.

about.title: # About Me (v{version:string})
gutsSection: I'm built with the [discord.js](https://discord.js.org) library, use [Prisma ORM](https://prisma.io) to persist data, cache data with [Redis](https://redis.io), and I run on the [Bun](https://bun.sh) runtime ({bunVersion:string}).

I'm currently hosted on my creator's [Raspberry Pi 5](https://raspberrypi.com/products/raspberry-pi-5) running [Ubuntu Server 24.04](https://ubuntu.com/server) ({hostname:string}). My hardware's uptime is {systemUptime:string} and I have been running for {serviceRuntime:string}.
dataSourceSection: ## Where do you get your data?
I get all of my info from the [National Weather Service API](https://weather.gov/documentation/services-web-api) and the [U.S. Geological Survey API](https://usgs.gov/products/web-tools/apis).
aiUsageSection: **Nope**! I neither use AI to parse or transform data nor is any of my code written with AI. All of my code is written by a human, mistakes and all!

created: {channel:string} should now have a placeholder message sent to it that will be periodically updated with the desired radar image loop.
To remove this designation, simply delete the message at any time.
